steps.title: Identify the key clue
steps.content: The passage explains vegetation's two mechanisms for reducing pollution (deposition and dispersion), suggesting green space should solve urban air quality problems. But Venter et al. recommend “reducing anthropogenic emissions remain the primary focus”—a conclusion implying the study found a critical limitation to green space.

steps.title: Match the finding
steps.content: B identifies the precise problem: green space shows moderate overall benefit, but dense tree cover on busy streets blocks ventilation—the more effective dispersion mechanism—making green space ineffective or counterproductive exactly where pollution is worst. This failure explains why emissions reduction must be primary. The other findings either report reduced effectiveness without mechanism (A), suggest a solution through vegetation selection (C), or blame external weather (D). That matches choice B — B is correct.
